title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
Insurance for NRIs – How to choose?
Added Word Count: 361
URL: https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/life/insurance-for-nris.html
Non-resident Indians (NRIs) are allowed to buy life insurance policies in India. Thanks to the digital mode, they can easily buy the plan from the country where they live and get insured against the risk of premature demise. Moreover, with the different types of life insurance plans available, NRIs can –
Create a corpus for their financial goals
Get tax benefits
Diversify their portfolio by investing in Unit Linked Insurance Plans (ULIPs)
Get financial security for their loved ones
When it comes to buying insurance for NRIs, there are multiple options available in the market. Different insurers offer a range of insurance plans, and NRIs are spoilt for choice. In such a case, how can they choose a suitable policy?
There are some ways in which NRIs can choose a suitable life insurance policy for themselves. These ways are discussed below –
How Can NRIs Choose The Suitable Life Insurance Policy?
Comparison can help you find the suitable life insurance policy from the multiple options available in the market. However, before you compare, there are some things that you should keep in mind to find the right insurance for NRIs. These things are as follows –
1. Your financial goals
Your financial goals helps you determine the type of policy you want. Given the different types of life insurance plans available, you can find the suitable plan depending on your financial goals. For instance –
If you want to create a corpus for your child’s education and/or marriage, a child insurance plan may be apt
For retirement planning goals, a pension plan may make sense
If protection is your primary need, you may opt for a term insurance plan
For wealth creation goals, you may choose ULIPs or traditional life insurance plans like endowment and money-back.
2. Priority of the goals and the corpus required
Once you have identified your goals, list down their priority, i.e., which goal needs to be met first and which is a long-term one. This will help you prioritise your life insurance purchases too. For instance, if protection is a priority, you may first buy a term insurance plan. Thereafter, when you get married and start a family, you may choose a child insurance plan.
After prioritising your goals, assess the corpus needed for fulfilling them. This would help you choose a suitable sum assured for the policy that you buy. When estimating the corpus, try and factor in inflation so that you can plan optimally.
3. Existing assets and liabilities
If you have existing assets, they can pay for your financial goals. As such, you can reduce their value from the aggregate corpus needed for your goals. Similarly, if you have liabilities, you need to factor in their repayment in your absence. As such, their value should be added to the estimated corpus.
For instance, say you want to save up for your child’s marriage which you estimate would cost ₹50 lakhs. You have existing investments worth ₹20 lakhs and no liabilities. In this case, you may want to choose a sum assured of ₹30 lakhs when buying the policy. On the other hand, if you have existing liabilities of ₹10 lakhs, a corpus of ₹40 lakhs would be needed to pay off the liabilities and also meet the goal.
So, assess your portfolio and figure out the optimal sum assured for the life insurance plan.
4. Number of dependents
The number of financial dependents would also affect the choice of the sum assured. If a lot of family members depend on you financially, you would need a higher sum assured and vice versa.
You can use the life insurance calculator, an online tool, and assess the optimal sum assured basis your financial goals.
Key Parameters to Compare Before Choosing an Insurance Plan
Once you have identified your needs and know how to choose NRI life insurance, the next step is to compare the available plans and choose the suitable one. To compare, here are some parameters that can help you find a suitable plan –
Coverage offered:
Check out the coverage benefits of the policy. Besides covering premature demise, find out the additional features of the policy like bonus, guaranteed additions, add-on riders, income options, etc.
You can choose a policy that offers suitable coverage benefits so that you get a comprehensive scope of protection.
Riders available:
Riders can enhance the scope of the policy. Look for the available riders and choose those which align with your coverage needs.
Premium rates:
Compare the premium rates along with the coverage of the policy and not independently. This is because a policy with affordable premiums might offer low coverage too. The preferable policy would be the one that offers competitive premiums without compromising on the coverage benefits.
Claim process and history:
Lastly, check the claim process of the insurance company. Since you are living abroad, a digitised and simple claim process is feasible so that claims can be made easily. So, look for companies that offer a simplified claim process which can be followed online for ease and convenience.
Secondly, check the company’s past claim record which can be found through its Claim Settlement Ratio (CSR). The ratio denotes the percentage of claims settled against the total claims made.
Tax Benefits in India & Abroad
Indian life insurance policies offer tax benefits on the premiums paid as well as the benefits received. Understand the rules of income tax for NRIs. Check the Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA) between India and the resident country to avoid paying double tax on the same income.
Premium Payment Options
NRI life insurance plans offer different premium payment options like single premium, limited premium or regular premiums. You can choose a premium payment term that aligns with your budget and is affordable.
Regulatory Compliance
Understand the regulatory requirements of India and the country in which you reside. It is important to fulfil the regulatory conditions when buying life insurance in India as an NRI.
Key takeaways
NRIs can buy life insurance plans in India for financial security and also to create a corpus for their financial goals.
To choose the right plan, assess your financial goals, their timeline, existing assets and liabilities, and the number of dependents you have.
It is better to compare and buy a suitable life insurance policy based on the coverage offered, available riders, premium rates, and claim process.
Understand the tax benefits of life insurance plans, too, and ensure that the premiums are affordable.
Wrapping Up
Life insurance is an important financial tool, whether you are an NRI or a resident individual. It can help you plan for your financial goals and also provide financial security to your family in your absence. So, use the aforementioned tips and find a suitable policy for your coverage needs. You would be able to fulfil your financial goals, whether they are a child’s higher education or retirement planning for NRIs.
FAQs
Can NRIs buy life insurance policies in India?
Yes, NRIs are allowed to buy life insurance policies in India as per the provisions of the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA).
Do NRIs need to be physically present in India to buy insurance?
No, NRIs do not need to be physically present in India. They can buy a life insurance policy online from their resident country.
Is a medical examination mandatory for NRIs while buying insurance?
The requirement of medical examination depends on the type of policy, the coverage, the age of the NRI and medical history. Insurance companies might require NRIs to undergo medical check-ups when buying life insurance.
Can NRIs pay premiums in foreign currency?
NRIs can pay premiums in foreign currency through their NRE or NRO bank account.
Disclaimer
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. Insurance for NRIs – How to choose?

URL: https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/life/insurance-for-nris.html
Non-resident Indians (NRIs) are allowed to buy life insurance policies in India. ● Get financial security for their loved ones

There are some ways in which NRIs can choose a suitable life insurance policy for themselves. These ways are discussed below –

1. Your financial goals
Your financial goals helps you determine the type of policy you want. Given the different types of life insurance plans available, you can find the suitable plan depending on your financial goals. For instance –
● If you want to create a corpus for your child’s education and/or marriage, a child insurance plan may be apt
● For retirement planning goals, a pension plan may make sense
● If protection is your primary need, you may opt for a term insurance plan
● For wealth creation goals, you may choose ULIPs or traditional life insurance plans like endowment and money-back.
2. Priority of the goals and the corpus required
Once you have identified your goals, list down their priority, i.e., which goal needs to be met first and which is a long-term one. This will help you prioritise your life insurance purchases too. After prioritising your goals, assess the corpus needed for fulfilling them. This would help you choose a suitable sum assured for the policy that you buy. When estimating the corpus, try and factor in inflation so that you can plan optimally.
3. Existing assets and liabilities
If you have existing assets, they can pay for your financial goals. As such, you can reduce their value from the aggregate corpus needed for your goals. Similarly, if you have liabilities, you need to factor in their repayment in your absence. As such, their value should be added to the estimated corpus.
For instance, say you want to save up for your child’s marriage which you estimate would cost ₹50 lakhs. You have existing investments worth ₹20 lakhs and no liabilities. In this case, you may want to choose a sum assured of ₹30 lakhs when buying the policy. On the other hand, if you have existing liabilities of ₹10 lakhs, a corpus of ₹40 lakhs would be needed to pay off the liabilities and also meet the goal.
So, assess your portfolio and figure out the optimal sum assured for the life insurance plan.
4. Number of dependents
The number of financial dependents would also affect the choice of the sum assured. If a lot of family members depend on you financially, you would need a higher sum assured and vice versa.
You can use the life insurance calculator, an online tool, and assess the optimal sum assured basis your financial goals.
Key Parameters to Compare Before Choosing an Insurance Plan
Once you have identified your needs and know how to choose NRI life insurance, the next step is to compare the available plans and choose the suitable one. To compare, here are some parameters that can help you find a suitable plan –
1. Coverage offered:
Check out the coverage benefits of the policy. Besides covering premature demise, find out the additional features of the policy like bonus, guaranteed additions, add-on riders, income options, etc.
You can choose a policy that offers suitable coverage benefits so that you get a comprehensive scope of protection.
2. Riders available:
Riders can enhance the scope of the policy. Look for the available riders and choose those which align with your coverage needs.
3. Premium rates:
Compare the premium rates along with the coverage of the policy and not independently. This is because a policy with affordable premiums might offer low coverage too. The preferable policy would be the one that offers competitive premiums without compromising on the coverage benefits.
4. Claim process and history:
Lastly, check the claim process of the insurance company. Since you are living abroad, a digitised and simple claim process is feasible so that claims can be made easily. So, look for companies that offer a simplified claim process which can be followed online for ease and convenience.
Secondly, check the company’s past claim record which can be found through its Claim Settlement Ratio (CSR). The ratio denotes the percentage of claims settled against the total claims made.
5. Premium Payment Options
Life insurance plans offer different premium payment options like single premium, limited premium or regular premiums. You can choose a premium payment term that aligns with your budget and is affordable.
Key takeaways
● NRIs can buy life insurance plans in India for financial security and also to create a corpus for their financial goals. ● To choose the right plan, assess your financial goals, their timeline, existing assets and liabilities, and the number of dependents you have. ● It is better to compare and buy a suitable life insurance policy based on the coverage offered, available riders, premium rates, and claim process. ● Understand the tax benefits of life insurance plans
Wrapping Up
Life insurance is an important financial tool, whether you are an NRI or a resident individual. It can help you plan for your financial goals and also provide financial security to your family in your absence. So, use the aforementioned tips and find a suitable policy for your coverage needs. You would be able to fulfil your financial goals, whether they are a child’s higher education or retirement planning for NRIs.
FAQs
1. Can NRIs buy life insurance policies in India?
Yes, NRIs are allowed to buy life insurance policies in India as per the provisions of the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA).
2. Do NRIs need to be physically present in India to buy insurance?
No, NRIs do not need to be physically present in India. They can buy a life insurance policy online from their resident country.
3. Is a medical examination mandatory for NRIs while buying insurance?
The requirement of medical examination depends on the type of policy, the coverage, the age of the NRI and medical history. Life Insurance companies might require NRIs to undergo medical check-ups when buying life insurance.
4. Can NRIs pay premiums in foreign currency?
NRIs can pay premiums in foreign currency through their NRE or NRO bank accounts, international credit cards, SWIFT transfers, and other modes.
